**Why**: Para mantener un historial accesible de decisiones clave y correcciones importantes, facilitando revisiones y aprendizajes futuros.

observations.title: Session summary: bitbucket-dashboard
observations.content:
## Goal
Build a secure Bitbucket dashboard web app (Go + HTMX) that fetches repository metrics and protects Bitbucket credentials using best practices.

## Instructions
- Keep tokens out of frontend JS/storage; store them securely in signed HttpOnly cookies.
- Support multiple Bitbucket workspaces and allow selecting repos via UI.
- Enforce security headers and optional HTTPS redirection.
- Provide clear docs + Docker support for easy deployment.

## Discoveries
- Go templates with ParseGlob("templates/**") fail on Windows; switched to WalkDir-based template discovery for test resilience.
- `securecookie` requires keys of 16/24/32 bytes; we now pad/truncate `AUTH_SECRET` to 32 bytes.
- Adding `FORCE_HTTPS=true` config allows safe deployment behind reverse proxies without changing app code.

## Accomplished
-  Implemented secure login flow with token stored in signed HttpOnly cookie (`internal/web/auth.go`).
-  Added cookie expiration (1h) and secure flag based on request security.
-  Added security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.) and optional HTTP->HTTPS redirect.
-  Added Dockerfile + docker-compose for easy deployment.
-  Updated README with security instructions and docker usage.
-  Added/configured `AUTH_SECRET`, `FORCE_HTTPS`, multiple workspace support.
-  All Go tests pass (`go test ./...`).

## Relevant Files
- internal/web/auth.go  secure cookie handling + token storage.
- internal/web/server.go  handlers, security middleware, HTTPS enforcement.
- internal/config/config.go  env config parsing + force HTTPS flag.
- templates/login.html  secure login form.
- Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml  containerization support.
- README.md  updated docs for secure deployment.

observations.title: Session summary: bitbucket-dashboard-mcp
observations.content:
## Goal
Crear un servidor MCP (Model Context Protocol) para integrar Bitbucket y Jira en el proyecto bitbucket-dashboard.

## Instructions
- Los servidores MCP deben estar en carpetas dentro del proyecto principal
- Usar Go como lenguaje base para mantener consistencia con el proyecto
- Siempre incluir ejemplos de configuracin (.env.example)
- Soportar tanto ejecucin local como en Docker

## Discoveries
- El MCP Go requiere la librera `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` para comunicacin con el protocolo
- Las credenciales de Bitbucket se pueden obtener como "App Password" desde las configuraciones personales
- Jira usa API tokens que se generan en el portal de Atlassian (id.atlassian.com)
- Las APIs de Bitbucket y Jira usan autenticacin Basic Auth con usuario y token/password

## Accomplished
-  Creado estructura de directorio `/mcp` con subdirectorios `config/` y `tools/`
-  Implementado cliente Bitbucket (ListProjects, ListRepositories, ListPullRequests, AddComment)
-  Implementado cliente Jira (ListProjects, ListIssues, GetIssue, CreateIssue)
-  Servidor MCP principal (main.go) que expone herramientas para ambas plataformas
-  Configuracin modular (config/config.go) que carga variables de entorno
-  Dockerfile y docker-compose.yml para containerizacin
-  Documentacin completa en README.md con instrucciones de configuracin

## Relevant Files
- [mcp/main.go](mcp/main.go)  Servidor MCP principal con registro de herramientas
- [mcp/config/config.go](mcp/config/config.go)  Carga y valida configuracin de entorno
- [mcp/tools/bitbucket.go](mcp/tools/bitbucket.go)  Cliente y mtodos para API de Bitbucket
- [mcp/tools/jira.go](mcp/tools/jira.go)  Cliente y mtodos para API de Jira
- [mcp/docker-compose.yml](mcp/docker-compose.yml)  Orquestacin Docker
- [mcp/.env.example](mcp/.env.example)  Template de configuracin
- [mcp/README.md](mcp/README.md)  Gua de uso y configuracin

observations.title: Session summary: workspace-foundation

observations.title: Session summary: ai-dev-platform
observations.content:
## Goal
Created comprehensive AI Development Platform proposal document for executive and technical stakeholders.

## Instructions
- Project must be 100% English (Spanish only in chat)
- No emojis in code/scripts - allowed only in documentation for diagrams
- Language of proposal: Spanish (business and technical)
- Must be complete enough to present to board and technical leads

## Discoveries
- Proposal needs dual-track structure: business language (ROI, KPIs) for executives + technical depth (APIs, data models) for tech leads
- Important to include: ROI calculation, implementation timeline, risks, alternatives comparison
- Platform architecture matches what workspace-foundation already does + central governance layer
- 3 key differentiators: multi-cloud, policies-as-code, custom integration

observations.title: Session summary: bitbucket-dashboard
observations.content:
## Goal
Optimize and fix the dashboard metrics view and server stability.

## Instructions
- Use Go 1.21+ standards.
- Maintain consistent template naming (`metrics.html` for `ExecuteTemplate`).
- Keep environment configuration in `.env`.
- Ensure server processes are cleaned up properly.

## Discoveries
- The `metrics_view.html` was failing because the template name in `ExecuteTemplate` (`metrics.html`) didn't match the `{{define "metrics_view.html"}}` block.
- Added a `substr` function to `template.FuncMap` to fix template rendering errors where string slicing was needed.
- `Invoke-WebRequest` was timing out because some API calls were too slow; optimized HTTP client timeouts from 20s to 10s.
- The server often left hanging processes on port 8083, necessitating robust cleanup scripts.

## Accomplished
-  Fixed template naming mismatch (`metrics.html`).
-  Implemented `substr` function in Go template engine.
-  Optimized HTTP client timeout to 10 seconds.
-  Restored original template structure.
-  Verified dashboard metrics endpoint (`/metrics`) returns 200 OK.

## Next Steps
-  Continue testing dashboard functionality with different repos.
-  Polish UI/UX based on metrics visual accuracy.
-  Address any remaining API rate limiting if observed.

## Relevant Files
- internal/web/server.go  Updated template functions and handler.
- internal/bitbucket/client.go  Optimized client HTTP timeout.
- templates/partials/metrics.html  Restored and fixed template definition.

observations.title: Fixed JSON unmarshaling error in PR summary
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed JSON unmarshaling error by updating `prSummary` struct to correctly handle the nested `author` object from the Bitbucket API.
**Why**: The Bitbucket API changed/was incorrectly mapped, returning an object instead of a string for the `author` field, causing a 500 error on the `/metrics` endpoint.
**Where**: `internal/bitbucket/client.go` (updated struct), `internal/bitbucket/repository_adapter.go` (updated mapping logic).
**Learned**: Always define complex API response fields as structs rather than primitive types if the API schema suggests nested objects.

observations.content: Full startup should remain reliable without optional demo scripts; demos should be toggled by configuration flags.

-  Updated `push` command guidance in both repos to show exact command sequence for push+PR vs push-only.
-  Updated help text/examples in both repos to document new publish flow options.
-  Created and pushed branch `feature/publish-mode-push-pr-selector` in both repos.

**Why**: User reported root script drift and requested strict adherence to naming/path governance to avoid inconsistency.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/setup.sh (moved), workspace-foundation/scripts/foundation/setup.sh, workspace-foundation/docs/getting-started/CROSS-PLATFORM-SETUP.md, workspace-foundation/docs/sdd/foundation-sdd.md, workspace-foundation/docs/reference/script-registry.md, workspace-foundation/scripts/foundation/README.md.

observations.title: Published setup relocation to main
observations.content:
**What**: Executed full publish flow for workspace-foundation: committed setup script relocation, merged feature branch into develop (PR #9), then merged develop into main (PR #10).
**Why**: User requested to complete publication and ensure all changes are impacted on the primary branch.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/scripts/foundation/setup.sh, workspace-foundation/docs/getting-started/CROSS-PLATFORM-SETUP.md, workspace-foundation/docs/sdd/foundation-sdd.md, workspace-foundation/docs/reference/script-registry.md, workspace-foundation/scripts/foundation/README.md.

Also documented this behavior in the orchestrator skill, AGENTS policy, developer communication policy, workflow help, orchestrator guidance, and pre-push advisory checks.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Homologar y automatizar la actualizacin de , engram y native-tools en workspace-foundation, aclarando si brew es requisito en Windows.

## Instructions
- Mantener flujo GitFlow completo hasta main.
- Dejar la gua clara para Windows sin dependencia de Homebrew.
- Automatizar en scripts/workflow, no solo documentar.

## Discoveries
- brew no est disponible en PowerShell/Windows por defecto; no es requisito para foundation.
-  en Windows se instala va repo + `bash install.sh` (Git Bash), no por `go install`.
- Al parchear scripts bash desde PowerShell, UTF-8 con BOM rompe shebang en Git Bash; usar UTF-8 sin BOM.
- Hubo conflictos de merge en `wf.ps1` al promover feature->develop y develop->main; la nica divergencia relevante fue ValidateSet con `update-tools`.

## Accomplished
-  Actualizadas herramientas locales:  v2.8.1, engram 1.12.0, native-tools 1.20.0.
-  Creado `scripts/utilities/update-tools.ps1` para actualizar toolchain completo sin brew.
-  Integrado comando `update-tools` en `scripts/utilities/wf.ps1` y agregado en help/dispatch.
-  Actualizado `scripts/utilities/update-suite.ps1` para delegar a `update-tools.ps1`.
-  Actualizada documentacin en README y guas de instalacin/prerrequisitos para Windows.
-  Publicado en GitHub: PR #11 (feature->develop) y PR #12 (develop->main) ambos MERGED.

## Relevant Files
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/update-tools.ps1  nuevo actualizador automatizado de /engram/native-tools.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/wf.ps1  nuevo comando `update-tools` + integracin en `update`.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/update-suite.ps1  delegacin al script nico de actualizacin.
- workspace-foundation/README.md  comandos y nota explcita de no-brew en Windows.
- workspace-foundation/docs/getting-started/PREREQUISITES.md  instalacin correcta de  + update one-shot.
- workspace-foundation/docs/getting-started/DEVELOPER-SETUP.md  flujo corregido de instalacin de .
- workspace-foundation/docs/getting-started/installation.md  tabla homologada con install real y comando de actualizacin.

observations.title: Engram-tool synced to upstream main
observations.content:
**What**: Audited `engram-tool`, discarded local manual changes, and hard-synced local `main` to `origin/main` at commit `64bf163`.
**Why**: User expected native/upstream-clean state and was seeing manual-change indicators.
**Where**: workspace `engram-tool` git state; local native binary at `C:/Users/emman/bin/engram.exe`.
**Learned**: The repository copy `engram-tool/engram.exe` can still report old local-v1.9.4 messaging even when the active installed CLI is updated (`engram 1.12.0` in GOPATH/bin / PATH). Source of truth for runtime should be PATH binary, not repo-root executable.

observations.title: Engram-tool optional for runtime
observations.content:
**What**: Confirmed foundation and dashboard resolve Engram from PATH/GOBIN/GOPATH and install via go install if missing; they do not depend on local source repo engram-tool for runtime.
**Why**: User asked whether engram-tool clone is required when global engram is already updated.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/run-engram.ps1, workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/clean-runtime.ps1, bitbucket-dashboard/scripts/utilities/run-engram.ps1.
**Learned**: engram-tool is useful only for contributing to Engram source, local debugging, or pinning custom branches; not required for normal workspace operation.

observations.title: Removed engram-tool as local dependency
observations.content:
**What**: Removed local `engram-tool` directory and removed explicit `engram-tool` references from foundation docs and runtime cleanup script.
**Why**: Avoid confusion about requirements; foundation/dashboard use global/path-based `engram` binary, not a local source checkout.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/docs/guides/runtime-state.md, workspace-foundation/docs/reference/tools.md, workspace-foundation/docs/reference/structure.md, workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/clean-runtime.ps1; local folder C:/Workspace_local/engram-tool deleted.
**Learned**: Keep docs/tooling language focused on runtime contract (`engram` in PATH + isolated ENGRAM_DATA_DIR) and avoid naming optional local clones as if they were required components.

observations.title: Engram references pinned to official upstream
observations.content:
**What**: Updated foundation and dashboard references so Engram installation/help text points consistently to official upstream `github.com/workspace-foundation/engram` and removed config behavior that cloned a local Engram source repo before install.
**Why**: User requested avoiding confusion with local tool clones and requiring references to the original author source only.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/config/workspace.config.json, workspace-foundation/config/workspace.portable.example.json, workspace-foundation/docs/reference/structure.md, workspace-foundation/docs/getting-started/installation.md, workspace-foundation/README.md, bitbucket-dashboard/docs/getting-started/prerequisites.md, bitbucket-dashboard/scripts/utilities/run-engram.ps1
**Learned**: Keeping install commands direct (`go install ...@latest`) is the clearest contract and avoids implying local source clones are required.

## Goal
Confirm and publish all updates so Engram references point only to the official upstream source and avoid local-clone confusion.

## Instructions
- Validate scripts, automation, governance, and docs before publishing.
- Publish changes in both repositories to main (feature -> develop -> main).

## Discoveries

observations.content:
## Goal
Homologar Foundation y bitbucket-dashboard para que skills y orquestacin funcionen en modelo Foundation-first con actualizacin por sync.

## Instructions
- Mantener skills nuevos como nativos/autocontenidos (sin dependencia de references para operacin base).
- Flujo operativo: actualizar en Foundation, publicar, consumidores actualizan con foundation-sync.
- Corregir comandos/documentacin para que coincidan con switches reales de PowerShell.

## Discoveries
- `wf.ps1 foundation-sync` en consumidor falla si faltan `config/foundation-sync.json` o `scripts/utilities/foundation-sync.ps1`.
- El switch correcto para PowerShell en este flujo es `-CreatePr` (no `--create-pr`).
- El wrapper `wf.ps1` puede quedar inconsistente si bootstrap/sync files no estn presentes localmente.
- Diagnsticos de `get_errors` pueden mostrar advertencias stale aunque el archivo ya fue actualizado; conviene validar con lectura directa de lnea.

## Accomplished
-  Se actualiz poltica de distribucin y activacin on-demand en `skills/SKILL_INDEX.md` y `skills/project-orchestrator-skill/SKILL.md` en Foundation.
-  Se corrigieron referencias de comandos invlidos en skills (`context-engineering-skill`, `sdd-skill`).
-  Se eliminaron secciones de references en skills importados clave para que sean autocontenidos.
-  Se restaur/cre en dashboard el contrato de sync (`config/foundation-sync.json`) con assets de scripts y skills compartidos.
-  Se agreg `scripts/utilities/foundation-sync.ps1` en dashboard y se ejecut apply/check hasta estado limpio (`up-to-date=30`, `to-update=0`).
-  Se verific que no hay coincidencias de `references/` en los 7 skills nuevos homologados (Foundation + dashboard).
-  Se limpi una asignacin no usada en `workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/wf.ps1` sin cambiar comportamiento.
-  Pendiente publicar cambios (commits/PR) en ambos repos con orden Foundation primero, luego consumidor.

## Relevant Files
- workspace-foundation/skills/SKILL_INDEX.md  poltica Foundation-first/on-demand y expansin del catlogo.
- workspace-foundation/skills/project-orchestrator-skill/SKILL.md  contrato operativo y mapeo de deteccin actualizado.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/foundation-sync.ps1  mensaje/flujo de sync y PR switch.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/wf.ps1  integracin de `foundation-sync` y ajustes de comandos.
- bitbucket-dashboard/config/foundation-sync.json  manifiesto consumidor con assets sincronizados.
- bitbucket-dashboard/scripts/utilities/foundation-sync.ps1  runner local de sync para apply/check.
- bitbucket-dashboard/scripts/utilities/wf.ps1  comando `foundation-sync` y flujo operativo actualizado.

## Goal
Formalizar, normalizar y documentar el criterio SDD recomendado con enforcement claro en Foundation.

## Instructions
- Dejar criterio actual vs recomendado explicitado en documentacin oficial.
- Alinear orquestador y skill SDD para que la poltica sea operativa y auditable.
- Publicar y mergear cambios para cerrar la formalizacin.

## Discoveries
- SDD ya estaba activo en skills, pero faltaba una poltica explcita de enforcement con excepciones y checklist de PR.
- No exista un punto de entrada estandarizado en docs/specs con plantilla reusable.
- El orquestador necesitaba separar explcitamente roles: orquestador (owner),  (compliance), native-tools (asistente).

## Accomplished
-  Se cre poltica formal en docs/reference/SDD-GOVERNANCE-POLICY.md.
-  Se agreg docs/specs/README.md y docs/specs/SPEC-TEMPLATE.md para estandarizar artefactos SDD.
-  Se actualiz skills/project-orchestrator-skill/SKILL.md con seccin de enforcement SDD y divisin de responsabilidades.
-  Se actualiz skills/sdd-skill/SKILL.md con perfil mandatory/conditional y manejo de excepciones mediante mini-spec.
-  Se actualiz skills/SKILL_INDEX.md y docs/README.md para trazabilidad y descubribilidad.
-  Publicado y mergeado en develop mediante PR #33.

## Relevant Files
- workspace-foundation/docs/reference/SDD-GOVERNANCE-POLICY.md  poltica oficial SDD.
- workspace-foundation/docs/specs/README.md  entrypoint de specs activos.
- workspace-foundation/docs/specs/SPEC-TEMPLATE.md  plantilla base de especificacin.

## Goal
Implementar enforcement automatico en CI para la gobernanza SDD en PRs de Foundation.

## Instructions
- Integrar el gate en workflow existente.
- Mantener comportamiento no destructivo: mandatory para feature/* y advisory para otras ramas.
- Documentar script nuevo en registry y diagnostics.

## Discoveries
- El workflow de referencia para integrar el gate era .github/workflows/script-governance.yml.
- La forma mas robusta de excepcion controlada es via body del PR: SDD-EXEMPT + SDD-EXEMPT-REASON.
- El check de estado de spec es viable desde CI usando GITHUB_EVENT_PATH y diff contra base ref.

## Accomplished
-  Se agrego scripts/diagnostics/validate-sdd-governance.ps1 con gate branch-aware.
-  Se integr paso bloqueante en .github/workflows/script-governance.yml para eventos pull_request.

-  Cambios publicados y mergeados a develop mediante PR #34.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Lanzar v1.0.0 como el primer release estable oficial de Gentleman Foundation, con governance formal.

## Instructions
- v1.0.0 es nuestro primer release "real" (no v3.0.0).
- Foundation es prioridad; Dashboard an no est listo para versionado.
- Usar `main` como rama de produccin, `develop` como integracin.
- Crear release con docs de estrategia, checklist, y migration.
- No crear rama principal nueva; usar estructura existente.

## Discoveries
- Foundation ya tena v2.2.1 como "pre-release", pero v1.0.0 es primer release formal.
- `main` es rama por defecto en GitHub; ya est establecida.
- Conflictos en `wf.ps1` entre develop y main debido a cambios divergentes en ambas ramas.
- Resolver conflictos usando `--ours` en main (mantener autoridad) y mergear hacia atrs.
- Tag v1.0.0 creado exitosamente en develop/main con GitHub Release oficial publicada.

## Accomplished
-  Creado `docs/guides/RELEASE-STRATEGY.md` con SemVer, deprecation policy, support matrix.
-  Creado `docs/guides/RELEASE-CHECKLIST.md` con validacin pre-release y ejecucin.
-  Creado `MIGRATION.md` para upgrade path desde v2.2.x.
-  Actualizado `CHANGELOG.md` a v1.0.0 - 2026-04-13 con todos los cambios de sesin.
-  Tag v1.0.0 creado y publicado en origin.
-  GitHub Release v1.0.0 publicada con notas completas.
-  Rama main sincronizada con develop (merge y push completo).
-  Estado limpio en ambas ramas base.

## Relevant Files
- CHANGELOG.md  v1.0.0 formal con fecha 2026-04-13
- docs/guides/RELEASE-STRATEGY.md  estrategia de versioning y soporteMigration.md  gua de upgrade
- docs/guides/RELEASE-CHECKLIST.md  checklist pre-release
- GitHub Release v1.0.0: https://github.com/EmmanuelOrtiz87/workspace-foundation/releases/tag/v1.0.0

observations.title: Governance decision framework temporary doc
observations.content:
**What**: Created a temporary governance/decision framework document consolidating directives, consultation boundaries, accepted defaults, double-validation criteria, omission rationale, automated decision logic, and improvement plan.
**Why**: User requested a complete inventory of rules/definitions and a future-facing analysis artifact to evolve configuration and governance.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/docs/supplementary/TEMP-AI-GOVERNANCE-DECISION-FRAMEWORK-2026-04-14.md; sources reviewed include .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md, workspace-foundation/AGENTS.md, config/orchestrator.json, config/structure-policy.json, config/workspace.config.json, tools/session-autostart.config.json, scripts/utilities/ensure-tools-active.ps1, scripts/utilities/wf.ps1, scripts/diagnostics/validate-script-governance.ps1.
**Learned**: Effective decision behavior is not encapsulated only by orchestrator skill + Engram; it is jointly governed by runtime instructions, workspace/repo policies, config files, and executable validation gates.

observations.title: Governance doc extended with coverage and portability
observations.content:
**What**: Extended temporary governance framework with explicit coverage marks (100%/Partial/Pending), canonicalization plan for future releases, lessons learned, activation model, and portability/knowledge-sharing guidance.
**Why**: User requested concrete clarity on productivity value, maturity trend, auto-applicability, and how knowledge transfers across repos/machines.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/docs/supplementary/TEMP-AI-GOVERNANCE-DECISION-FRAMEWORK-2026-04-14.md
**Learned**: The user prioritizes practical operational clarity over abstract governance; coverage marking improves decision confidence without forcing immediate canonical migration.

observations.title: Lecciones consolidadas ultimas 2 sesiones
observations.content:
**What**: Consolidated learnings from the two most recent work sessions: toolchain homogenization, governance consistency, and decision-framework clarification.
**Why**: User requested explicit retention of recent operational knowledge before session closure.
**Where**: workspace-foundation scripts and docs, mainly scripts/utilities/ensure-tools-active.ps1, scripts/utilities/update-tools.ps1, scripts/utilities/wf.ps1, scripts/validation/check-updates.ps1, scripts/diagnostics/validate-script-governance.ps1, docs/supplementary/TEMP-AI-GOVERNANCE-DECISION-FRAMEWORK-2026-04-14.md.
**Learned**: 1) Using config/workspace.config.json as the single source of truth prevents drift across health/update/check scripts. 2) Governance quality depends on executable checks, not docs alone. 3) Operationally everything is active and functional; remaining gaps are hardening/formalization, not blockers. 4) Temporary governance docs are valuable for decision confidence before canonicalization. 5) Knowledge portability requires repo artifacts plus explicit memory capture and machine setup steps.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Close the session with complete persistence of recent governance and tooling learnings.

## Instructions
- Keep scope constrained to workspace-foundation and avoid normalizing to bitbucket-dashboard.
- Favor practical, evidence-based governance guidance over speculative process overhead.

## Discoveries
- Config-driven tool orchestration across health/update/check scripts materially reduced inconsistencies.
- The 5-tool model is now consistently represented in operational scripts and validation outcomes.
- Governance confidence improves when rules are linked to executable validators instead of narrative-only docs.
- The current platform is operationally ready; unfinished items are mainly formal hardening decisions.
- Repo-embedded knowledge transfers with git; external memory and machine environment state do not transfer automatically.

## Accomplished
-  Completed and pushed homogenization changes for tool lifecycle behavior across core scripts.
-  Removed a confirmed orphan script and kept referenced artifacts in place.
-  Produced and updated a temporary governance decision framework with coverage marks and portability guidance.
-  Captured consolidated learnings from the latest two sessions into Engram.
-  Canonical governance matrix and validator intentionally deferred pending team approval for future release.

## Relevant Files
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/ensure-tools-active.ps1  unified data-driven 5-tool health/install/re-check behavior.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/update-tools.ps1  added full 5-tool update/check coverage.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/wf.ps1  removed duplicate update execution and aligned help.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/validation/check-updates.ps1  corrected install guidance and tool coverage.
- workspace-foundation/docs/supplementary/TEMP-AI-GOVERNANCE-DECISION-FRAMEWORK-2026-04-14.md  temporary analysis artifact with implementation coverage and future canonicalization note.

observations.title: Automated day-end closure architecture
observations.content:
**What**: Designed and implemented automated day-end closure workflow with dual modes: manual (`wf.ps1 day-end-closure`) and scheduled/automatic (future via tasks or hooks).
**Why**: Prevent knowledge loss at shift end; ensure learnings are captured to Engram automatically; enable seamless session resume next day with full context restoration.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/day-end-closure.ps1 (orchestrator), scripts/utilities/end-session.ps1 (operational checks), scripts/utilities/wf.ps1 (CLI entry), docs/guides/DAY-END-CLOSURE.md (documentation).
**Learned**: Daily closure must be both opt-in (manual command) and opt-out (automatic on shell exit or scheduled); the two-stage model (operational + memory) ensures durability and auditability; artifacts in docs/sessions/ provide audit trail for retrospectives.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Implement automated day-end closure workflow to capture learnings, validate state, and persist memory without manual intervention.

## Instructions
- Dual-mode design: automatic execution + manual fallback option
- Full traceability: session tracked from start to close
- No manual commands required for daily closure after setup

## Discoveries
- day-end-closure requires four-stage pipeline: operational closure, workspace validation, memory capture, report generation
- Engram integration is ready at script level; actual command execution deferred to production
- Manual command (`wf.ps1 day-end-closure`) is sufficient for today; scheduler setup is documented for future activation

## Accomplished
-  Created scripts/utilities/day-end-closure.ps1  four-stage orchestrator with skip flags
-  Created docs/guides/DAY-END-CLOSURE.md  complete user guide with methods (manual, scheduled-task, profile-hook)
-  Updated wf.ps1 to register 'day-end-closure' as CLI command
-  Validated command registration and file staging
-  Committed and pushed commit 5d45e7b to main
-  Captured architectural decision in Engram
-  Automatic 24/7 execution deferred pending user preference on trigger method (shell-exit vs scheduled-task vs manual daily habit)

## Relevant Files

observations.title: session-2026-04-14-03 tool install fixes
observations.content: Fixed 3 real-world tool install issues in ensure-tools-active.ps1 + update-tools.ps1. (1)  binary detection post-install: Confirm-ToolAfterInstall now checks ~\bin, ~\.local\bin, Git/usr/bin and auto-adds to session PATH. (2) opencode 403: Install-Tool and Check-Opencode now catch HTTP 403 as optional-blocked, show 4 alternatives (GitHub releases, winget, npm, manual), does not fail bootstrap. (3) Version staleness: Added Get-GitHubLatestVersion + Compare-Version + Get-InstalledVersion helpers; Update-Engram/Update-nativeAi/Update- all compare installed vs latest before installing; go install uses GOFLAGS=-mod=mod to bypass stale module cache. Commit d1fa6f7 pushed.

observations.title: Cross-platform PowerShell tooling refactor
observations.content:
**What**: Refactored ensure-tools-active.ps1 and update-tools.ps1 to detect platform dynamically (windows/linux/macos), resolve platform-specific install metadata from config, remove hardcoded Windows-only shell/path assumptions, and validate with runtime smoke tests.
**Why**: User requested full agnosticism across OS/shell/CLI dependencies; audit found several `.windows` key accesses and fixed Windows path assumptions.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/ensure-tools-active.ps1, scripts/utilities/update-tools.ps1
**Learned**: In PowerShell conditionals, function calls used with operators should be wrapped as `(Get-CurrentPlatform)` to avoid passing `-eq/-ne` as function arguments. Also, PS5.1 is sensitive to encoding/non-ASCII artifacts, so keeping scripts ASCII-friendly and UTF-8 BOM avoids parser issues.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local

**Where**: docs/guides/TOOL-ACTIVATION.md, docs/getting-started/CROSS-PLATFORM-SETUP.md, docs/reference/tools.md, docs/getting-started/PREREQUISITES.md, scripts/utilities/update-tools.ps1

observations.title: Normalized docs language and PowerShell wording
observations.content:
**What**: Normalized mixed Spanish/English wording in active onboarding docs and visible script messages, and clarified that PowerShell is the canonical automation runtime rather than the only supported entrypoint.
**Why**: The user noticed mixed-language documentation and ambiguous wording that could misrepresent the stack as PowerShell-only instead of cross-platform with routed entrypoints.
**Where**: docs/getting-started/STACK-SETUP.md, docs/reference/SUITE-OVERVIEW.md, docs/getting-started/CROSS-PLATFORM-SETUP.md, scripts/git-hooks/run-engram.sh, scripts/git-hooks/clean-runtime.sh, scripts/foundation/bootstrap-workspace.ps1, scripts/utilities/orchestrator-status.ps1
**Learned**: The docs should consistently state 'PowerShell-canonical, wrapper-friendly, OS-aware' to avoid overclaiming full shell neutrality while still communicating strong portability.

observations.title: Removed powershell.exe from core workflow scripts
observations.content:
**What**: Refactored core workflow scripts to stop spawning `powershell.exe` for sibling PowerShell scripts and instead invoke them directly from the current PowerShell host. Also fixed `update-all.ps1` dry-run forwarding and success detection when delegating to `update-tools.ps1`.
**Why**: Remaining platform bias in the workflow core came from nested `powershell.exe` calls, which weakened portability and miscommunicated the runtime model.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/wf.ps1, scripts/utilities/auto-init-dev-environment.ps1, hooks/pre-commit.ps1, hooks/post-checkout.ps1, scripts/validation/update-all.ps1
**Learned**: For PowerShell-to-PowerShell calls, direct script invocation is more portable and simpler than relaunching a specific host executable. Also, when delegating to a PowerShell script, `$?` is more reliable than `$LASTEXITCODE` for success checks in the current host.

observations.title: Repo-wide PowerShell runner cleanup
observations.content:
**What**: Removed remaining direct `powershell.exe` invocations from core and secondary PowerShell scripts, replaced them with direct script invocation or dynamic runner resolution, and fixed related bootstrap path issues uncovered during validation.
**Why**: The user requested repo-wide cleanup of legacy platform-specific references to improve portability and align implementation with the documented cross-platform model.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/wf.ps1, scripts/utilities/auto-init-dev-environment.ps1, hooks/pre-commit.ps1, hooks/post-checkout.ps1, scripts/validation/update-all.ps1, scripts/utilities/compact-start.ps1, templates/project-root/scripts/compact-start.ps1, scripts/utilities/day-end-closure.ps1, scripts/utilities/end-session.ps1, scripts/diagnostics/system-diagnostics.ps1, scripts/utilities/install-workspace-skills.ps1, scripts/utilities/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1, scripts/utilities/run-engram.ps1, scripts/utilities/run-native-tools.ps1, scripts/project/init-workspace.ps1, scripts/project/new-project.ps1, scripts/foundation/bootstrap-workspace.ps1, scripts/utilities/enable-optional-post-commit.ps1, templates/project-root/scripts/enable-optional-post-commit.ps1
**Learned**: Cleanup of host-specific invocations exposed a few unrelated but real path bugs in bootstrap/new-project flows. Fixing the runner layer and then validating end-to-end is the fastest way to surface those hidden defects.

observations.content:
## Goal
Cerrar dos gaps en workspace-foundation: documentar el comportamiento de foundation-sync y agregar una capa de custom rules para reglas tcnicas/negocio/review.

## Instructions
- No hacer push hasta que el usuario confirme el ltimo tema a revisar.
- Trabajar directamente sobre `main` en workspace-foundation (flujo actual sin PR).
- El usuario prefiere que todo quede homologado y cerrado antes de subir  no dejar cambios parciales.
- Formato `.md` obligatorio para todos los archivos de reglas  no libre albedro de formato.
- Convenciones de Rule ID: TECH-NNN, BIZ-NNN, REV-NNN.

## Discoveries
- `custom-rules.ps1` fall parser la primera vez por string literal `"[ERROR] $(...)"`  el `[` se interpreta como array subscript. Fix: usar concatenacin `"[ERROR] " + $var` en lugar de interpolacin.
- `wf.ps1` con `ValidateSet`: pasar `-Mode status` como named param falla; debe ser posicional `@('status')`.
- `foundation-sync.ps1` tena el comportamiento (SHA256, replace vs preserve-local, roles source/consumer) implementado pero completamente indocumentado para usuarios.
- Commits directamente a `main` en esta sesin  no aplica PR retroactivo, los cambios son pblicos desde el push.

## Accomplished
-  `docs/guides/FOUNDATION-SYNC.md`  gua completa de comportamiento sync (commit b7d6c29, pushed)
-  `scripts/utilities/custom-rules.ps1`  nuevo script Mode status/export, solo `.md`, README excluido (commit dc5c988, pushed)

observations.content: Relative dataRoot values in workspace-foundation must always be resolved against the workspace root, not the caller's current directory, to keep demos portable across machines.

observations.content: Missing .engram-data on a fresh machine is first-use state, not an error; status scripts should report it as informational because the launcher creates it lazily.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Set and enforce the lowest-detail startup chat mode, persist it as an architecture decision in Engram, keep override paths available, and close session with documentation.

## Instructions
- Keep startup behavior simple+ultra and chat-compact by default.
- Preserve ability to override via command/instruction/script.
- Publish and close session with artifacts recorded.

## Discoveries
- Added persistent `chat_response` config with `default_level` + `enforce_on_session_start` so startup behavior is deterministic.
- `start-session.ps1` now reads chat baseline config and applies `set-chat-level` directly when enforcement is enabled.
- Engram now contains explicit architecture decision (`architecture-decision:startup-chat-baseline`) and mode-change evidence (`communication-response-mode`).
- Day-end closure produced a SessionId rendering quirk in log output (`-SessionId`) but closure artifacts and memory saves completed.

## Accomplished
-  Enforced startup chat baseline (`chat-compact` => `simple+ultra`) in config and runtime scripts.
-  Updated docs and agent instructions to treat startup chat mode as architecture decision while preserving override paths.
-  Verified runtime output (`wf response-mode`) shows active/default chat baseline and enforcement flag.
-  Saved architecture decision and mode traces in Engram.
-  Published code changes and session closure artifacts to `main`.

## Relevant Files
- config/orchestrator.json  persistent chat baseline and architecture decision metadata.
- scripts/utilities/response-mode.ps1  preserves `chat_response`, applies chat-level defaults, and logs changes to Engram.
- scripts/utilities/start-session.ps1  startup auto-apply uses enforced chat baseline.
- docs/guides/DEVELOPER-COMMUNICATION-POLICY.md  architecture baseline and override paths documented.
- .github/copilot-instructions.md  chat-layer startup enforcement documented.
- AGENTS.md  policy summary aligned with startup compact baseline.
- docs/sessions/2026-04-14-202002-delivery-closure.md  session closure artifact.
- docs/sessions/closure-report-2026-04-14 202011.md  final closure report.

observations.title: stack-on-demand no longer truncates response config
observations.content:
**What**: Updated stack activation scripts to stop overwriting orchestrator.json with a minimal object; activation now preserves existing keys and only upserts runtime fields, adding simple+ultra/chat-compact defaults only when missing.
**Why**: Auto-start activation was erasing chat/response settings, breaking token-efficiency baseline enforcement.
**Where**: tools/stack-on-demand.ps1, workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/stack-on-demand.ps1, workspace-foundation/config/orchestrator.json (repaired via response-mode set-chat-level)
**Learned**: Re-running activation previously removed communication_response_mode, response_profiles, and chat_response; after fix, repeated activation preserves simple+ultra and chat-compact.

observations.title: Engram + simple-ultra operating model
observations.content:
**What**: Confirmed operating model where Engram provides persistent session/context continuity while default chat/response mode stays in simple+ultra (chat-compact) for token efficiency.
**Why**: User validated whether compression strategy hurts context quality and whether it is functionally/technically complementary.
**Where**: Workspace operational policy for session startup, response-mode behavior, and stack activation.
**Learned**: This combination is complementary when escalation remains allowed for high-risk/complex tasks; optimize defaults without hard-locking detail level.

observations.title: Strict baseline governance for response/chat
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented strict governance pattern enforcing simple+ultra+chat-compact as mandatory baseline, with controlled override path requiring explicit command and reason, plus validator checks across workspace-foundation and bitbucket-dashboard.
**Why**: User requested always-active defaults, no operation outside rule by default, and orchestrator-managed optimization with configurable commands.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/response-mode.ps1, workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/stack-on-demand.ps1, workspace-foundation/config/orchestrator.json, workspace-foundation/docs/guides/DEVELOPER-COMMUNICATION-POLICY.md, C:/Workspace_local/tools/stack-on-demand.ps1, C:/Workspace_local/tools/validate-session-stack.ps1
**Learned**: Hard baseline can coexist with command-level adaptability by requiring explicit override intent (AllowPolicyOverride + OverrideReason) and immediate baseline re-enforcement command.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Harden orchestrator governance to enforce token-efficient response/chat defaults and restore architecture documentation structure consistency.

## Instructions
- Keep baseline response/chat in simple + ultra (chat-compact).
- Enforce strict governance by default, with explicit override path only.
- Preserve documentation organization and canonical architecture references.

## Discoveries
- On-demand activation was overwriting `config/orchestrator.json` with a minimal payload, dropping response/chat policy keys.
- Strict baseline works safely if overrides are explicit (`-AllowPolicyOverride`) and traceable (`-OverrideReason`).
- Presets must align with strict baseline; otherwise preset application can conflict with policy enforcement.
- Session validator can reliably gate policy compliance across multiple projects.

## Accomplished
-  Added strict response policy enforcement and baseline command in `scripts/utilities/response-mode.ps1`.
-  Updated stack activation scripts to preserve existing config and upsert missing policy defaults (`tools/stack-on-demand.ps1`, `scripts/utilities/stack-on-demand.ps1`).
-  Added hard baseline checks in `tools/validate-session-stack.ps1` and validated READY (17/17).
-  Restored architecture directory entry docs and updated stale architecture references.
-  Documented controlled override workflow in communication policy guide.
-  Pending publication step: changes are present in working tree but not yet committed.

## Relevant Files
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/response-mode.ps1  strict baseline policy + controlled override + enforce-baseline command.
- tools/stack-on-demand.ps1  non-destructive activation + policy/workflow default upsert.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/stack-on-demand.ps1  local mirror of activation safety and defaults.
- tools/validate-session-stack.ps1  strict baseline validation checks for both projects.
- workspace-foundation/config/orchestrator.json  active strict policy and optimization defaults.
- workspace-foundation/docs/guides/DEVELOPER-COMMUNICATION-POLICY.md  documented no-free-mode default and override commands.
- workspace-foundation/docs/architecture/README.md  architecture hub entry.
- workspace-foundation/docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md  architecture entry-point file.
- workspace-foundation/docs/README.md  architecture section added.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/validation/validate-project.ps1  canonical architecture doc checks updated.
- workspace-foundation/AGENTS.md  architecture reference path corrected.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Publish all pending governance/documentation changes to remote and harden closeout policy so session end warns/blocks when changes are not uploaded.

## Instructions
- No pending uploads at close.
- Push now, and if required use PR/merge.
- Define orchestrator-aligned policy to avoid closing with unpublished changes.

## Discoveries
- `C:\Workspace_local` is not a git repo; publication scope is `workspace-foundation`.
- Branch was `main` and remote push was allowed directly, so PR/merge flow was not required.
- Existing closeout flow generated artifacts/checks but did not block unpublished state by default.

## Accomplished
-  Committed and pushed all pending `workspace-foundation` changes to GitHub (`4e34ecd`).
-  Added closeout publication guard in `scripts/utilities/end-session.ps1` with explicit override `-AllowUnpublishedClose`.
-  Documented policy in `docs/guides/DAY-END-CLOSURE.md`.
-  Repository left clean after push (`git status --short` empty).

## Relevant Files
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/end-session.ps1  blocks closure when uncommitted/unpublished changes exist unless explicit override.
- workspace-foundation/docs/guides/DAY-END-CLOSURE.md  publication policy and override documented.
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/response-mode.ps1  strict baseline + controlled overrides.
- workspace-foundation/config/orchestrator.json  strict response policy and workflow optimization defaults.
- workspace-foundation/docs/guides/DEVELOPER-COMMUNICATION-POLICY.md  controlled override workflow documented.
- workspace-foundation/docs/architecture/README.md  architecture hub.
- workspace-foundation/docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md  architecture entry-point path continuity.

observations.title: Framework Core v1.0 released - 4 features implemented
observations.content:
**What**: Released Framework Core v1.0 with 4 major features (FF-007 to FF-010)
**Why**: User requested framework maturity progression for token efficiency and automation
**Where**: workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/, docs/reference/, skills/multi-agent-registry/

## Features Released
1. **Agent Result Schema (FF-007)**: JSON structured output with lane_id, status, skills_loaded, token_estimate
2. **Skills Auto-Discovery (FF-009)**: Auto-detects 55 skills, generates keyword-based agent mapping
3. **Parallel Dispatch (FF-010)**: 3 modes (parallel/sequential/adaptive), risk-based concurrency (2-4 agents)
4. **Event Bus System (FF-011)**: 10 standard events, pub/sub pattern, history tracking

## Commands Added
- `.\wf.ps1 agent DEV "task" -AsJson`  Structured agent result
- `.\wf.ps1 skills discover|map|agents|validate`  Skills management
- `.\wf.ps1 dispatch "DEV,QA" "task" [-DryRun]`  Parallel execution
- `.\wf.ps1 events list|subscribe|emit|history`  Event automation

## Remaining Backlog (FF-011, FF-012)
- Plugin Architecture (extensibility contract)
- Unified Metrics Dashboard (Grafana integration)

## Learned
- PowerShell parameter binding requires careful argument construction in wrapper scripts
- Event bus data stored in .event-bus/ directory (added to .gitignore)

observations.title: 7-agent model: BA, SAD, DEV, QA, OPS, GOV, DOC
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented 7-agent specialization model for token-efficient orchestration
**Why**: User requested specialized agents to reduce orchestrator bottleneck
**Where**: skills/multi-agent-registry/SKILL.md, agent-router.ps1

## 7 Specialized Agents
| Agent | Role | Skills | Token Est |
|-------|------|--------|-----------|
| BA | Business Analyst | bdd-scenarios, documentation | ~2K |
| SAD | Solution Architect | architecture, api-design, databases | ~3K |
| DEV | Developer | angular, react, tailwind, zustand, zod, security | ~3K |
| QA | Quality Assurance | testing, playwright, pytest | ~2K |
| OPS | DevOps | docker, k8s, terraform, git-workflow | ~2K |
| GOV | Governance | observability, incident-response, security | ~2K |
| DOC | Documentation | sdd, bdd, github-pr | ~2K |

## Benefits
- Token savings: ~60% (20K vs 50K tokens/session)
- Orchestrator slim: ~5K tokens
- Each agent: ~2-3K tokens

## Skills Auto-Discovery
- 55 skills auto-detected
- Keyword-based mapping to agents
- Unmapped skills flagged for review

## Token Efficiency Pattern
1. Orchestrator: decompose task, route to agents
2. Agents: load only domain skills, execute focused
3. Results: structured JSON for merge/consolidation

observations.title: Judgment Day implemented in foundation QA
observations.content:
**What**: Judgment Day dual-review protocol implemented in foundation
**Why**: High-confidence adversarial review before merge, prevents blind spots
**Where**: skills/code-review-orchestrator-skill/, skills/multi-agent-registry/

**Components**:
1. code-review.ps1 - judgment-day mode with dual-review
2. wf.ps1 - routes review --scope judgment-day
3. judgment-day.ps1 - standalone script
4. multi-agent-registry - AGENT-QA owns judgment-day

**How it works**:
- Round N: Judge A + Judge B run in parallel (blind)
- Synthesis: Confirmed (both) | Suspect A | Suspect B
- Fix confirmed issues
- Re-judge (max 2 iterations)
- Escalate if still failing

**Commands**:
- wf review --scope judgment-day
- wf agent QA "judgment day on <target>"

observations.title: Session artifacts generation system
observations.content:
**What**: Created automated audit, budget, and telemetry file generation system for session and PR closures

**Why**: Provide visibility into AI usage, costs, and development activity for better project management and cost control

**Where**: 
- `workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/generate-session-artifacts.ps1` - Main session artifacts generator
- `workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/generate-pr-artifacts.ps1` - PR artifacts generator
- Integrated into `finalize-session.ps1`, `day-end-closure.ps1`, and `project-pre-push.ps1`

**Learned**: 
- File naming convention: `<report-name>_<yyyy-MM-dd-HHmmss>.json`
- Three artifact types: audit (activity), budget (costs), telemetry (performance)
- Automatic triggers: session close (manual/auto), PR creation/push
- Cost calculation: $10 USD per 1M tokens with $5 session / $10 PR alerts
- Artifacts stored in `.audit/` directory with subdirectories by type

**Learned**:
- PowerShell syntax: `$env:$var` is invalid; use `(Get-ChildItem "env:$var").Value`
- PowerShell splatting requires pre-assignment: `$args = $cmd.args; & $cmd @args`
- `-Verbose` is reserved parameter; use `-Detailed` instead
- Glob patterns in Test-Path need Get-ChildItem: `Get-ChildItem -Filter "*.ps1"`
- Script governance requires session start artifact in docs/sessions/

observations.title: 7-Dimension Code Review Protocol added to orchestrator
observations.content:
**What**: Added comprehensive 7-Dimension Code Review Protocol to project-orchestrator-skill with full agent mapping.

**Why**: Ensure orchestrator, agents, and subagents know how to operate all 7 dimensions (Security, Quality, Architecture, Testing, Documentation, API Design, Git Workflow).

**Where**: skills/project-orchestrator-skill/SKILL.md

**7-Dimension Protocol:**
1. [S] Security  AGENT-GOV (security-expert-skill)
2. [Q] Quality  AGENT-DEV (typescript, code-review)
3. [A] Architecture  AGENT-SAD (architecture-governance)
4. [T] Testing  AGENT-QA (testing-strategy, playwright, pytest)
5. [D] Documentation  AGENT-DOC (documentation-governance)
6. [API] API Design  AGENT-SAD (api-design-skill)
7. [G] Git Workflow  AGENT-GOV (git-workflow-skill)

**Coverage Rules:**
- PRE-COMMIT: `wf review --scope quick` (Security + Quality)
- PRE-MERGE: `wf review --scope full` (all 7)
- JUDGMENT DAY: adversarial dual-review before major releases

observations.title: absorbed into Foundation as native ai-review
observations.content:
**What**: Absorbed all  (Gentleman ) functionalities into Foundation as native capabilities.

**Why**: Make  features permanent and native to Foundation, eliminating external dependency for code review.

**Where**: 
- scripts/utilities/invoke-ai-review.ps1 (main -native script)
- skills/commit-hygiene-skill/ (migrated)
- skills/docs-alignment-skill/ (migrated)
- skills/shellcheck-standards-skill/ (migrated)
- skills/testing-coverage-skill/ (migrated)
- config/ai-review.example.json (config template)
- docs/reference/-ABSORPTION-PLAN.md (migration plan)

**-native Features:**
- Multi-provider support: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, GitHub, Bedrock
- Smart caching: Skip unchanged files
- PR review mode: Full PR diff review
- Config hierarchy: env > project > global
- Strict mode: Fail on ambiguous AI responses
- Pre-commit hook installation
- Cache management

**Commands:**
```powershell
invoke-ai-review.ps1 run          # Run review
invoke-ai-review.ps1 install      # Install hook
invoke-ai-review.ps1 config        # Show config
invoke-ai-review.ps1 run -PRMode  # PR review
```

**Coexistence:**  remains available externally; Foundation uses native implementation.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace-foundation
observations.content:
## Goal
Fix all critical and warning bugs identified by judgment-day adversarial review in workspace-foundation audit scripts, then ship v2.2.0.

## Instructions
- User selected "opcion 2": fix everything + push + PR + merge + release
- judgment-day protocol: MUST NOT push until Round 3 re-judgment passes clean
- branch-pr skill requires issue with `status:approved` before PR

## Discoveries
- `audit-sweep.ps1` was silently swallowing all output via `2>&1 | Out-Null`  CRITICAL bug, audits appeared to pass but never surfaced real errors
- `Test-OrphanedDocs` regex `\.md\)` was a literal string match, not a capture group  100% false positives
- `$matches` is a PS automatic variable; user-defined `$matches` in script scope shadows it  use `$mdLinkMatches`
- `Test-MarkdownLinks` / `Test-ReadmeReferences` used `-replace '\.\./'` which is fragile; replaced with `Resolve-PathSafe` using `[System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath`
- Broken links in `docs/guides/AUDIT-WORKFLOW.md`: `../skills/` from `docs/guides/` resolves to `docs/skills/` not root `skills/`  fix: `../../skills/`
- Broken link in `skills/SKILL_INDEX.md`: `../../docs/` goes above repo root  fix: `../docs/`
- `v2.2.0` tag was already created locally in a prior session; `git tag` failed 128 but `git push origin v2.2.0` succeeded
- wf.ps1 is at `workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/wf.ps1`, NOT `c:\Workspace_local\scripts\utilities\wf.ps1`

## Accomplished
-  Fixed audit-sweep.ps1: Resolve-PathSafe, single JSON invocation, OrphanedDocs regex, $mdLinkMatches, _shared in nonSkills, PSScriptRoot fallback
-  Fixed audit-workflow.ps1: standalone $WorkingDir, Resolve-PathSafe, deep switch case, single invocation
-  Fixed sync-local.ps1: idempotent Copy-SyncItem, removed duplicate flatten entry
-  Fixed wf.ps1: scope validation, exit propagation, deep mode
-  Fixed judgment-day/SKILL.md: iteration policy, WARNING taxonomy, duplicate escalation path
-  Homologated foundation-audit-skill/SKILL.md (3 locations: skills/, .copilot, .claude)
-  Fixed broken links in AUDIT-WORKFLOW.md and SKILL_INDEX.md
-  Round 3 validation: quick  0 issues, deep  0 issues
-  Issue #47, PR #48 (squash merge), tag v2.2.0, release published

## Relevant Files
- workspace-foundation/skills/foundation-audit-skill/scripts/audit-sweep.ps1  main audit engine
- workspace-foundation/skills/foundation-audit-skill/scripts/audit-workflow.ps1  orchestrator
- workspace-foundation/skills/foundation-audit-skill/scripts/sync-local.ps1  local copy sync
- workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/wf.ps1  audit CLI (lines ~1598-1655)
- workspace-foundation/skills/judgment-day/SKILL.md  adversarial review protocol
- workspace-foundation/skills/foundation-audit-skill/SKILL.md  skill docs
- workspace-foundation/docs/guides/AUDIT-WORKFLOW.md  workflow guide (link fixed)
- workspace-foundation/skills/SKILL_INDEX.md  skill index (link fixed)

observations.title: Session summary: workspace-foundation
observations.content:
## Goal
Close the day with published changes, enforce optional integration posture, and provide management metrics/documents for today.

## Instructions
- Keep Foundation robust and agnostic.
- Treat /native-tools/workspace-foundation-skills as optional integrations.
- Provide decision-ready metrics and document references for management.

## Discoveries
- Health/governance can remain green while optional integrations are missing, if classified properly in config and checks.
- Compatibility wrappers must stay in legacy paths to avoid breaking demos/docs/governance references.
- Artifact rotation can archive session-start artifacts before second-stage validation, causing advisory "missing session start" warnings in day-end closure.
- Todays token-guard trajectory stayed within budget posture (last projected value 27.5%).

## Accomplished
-  Published artifact retention and v2.1 feature set to `main` (`c6fb99c`).
-  Reclassified , native-tools, and workspace-foundation-skills as optional in tooling/governance/runtime/docs and pushed to `main` (`e683a36`).

-  Ran health and governance validations successfully after changes.
-  Executed day-end closure and generated todays management artifacts (audit + delivery + closure report).

## Next Steps

- Refine day-end closure order to avoid session-start being rotated before second validation stage.
- If desired, add an executive daily summary markdown generated automatically from todays audits + metrics.

## Relevant Files
- config/workspace.config.json  tool optionality model for /native-tools/workspace-foundation-skills.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace-foundation
observations.content:
## Goal
Automate live context-efficiency actions so the orchestrator proactively optimizes session starts, then publish all updates and close the session.

## Instructions
- Orchestrator should handle optimization automatically.
- Keep manual execution path available for users.
- Update documentation and publish all generated changes.

## Discoveries
- Live context-efficiency nudges are effective when injected before key workflow commands.
- Existing checkpoint date parsing in wf status needed strong DateTimeOffset typing to avoid runtime errors.
- Enabling auto compact-start on RED immediately records metrics and creates context artifacts during start-session.
- `.audit/` should remain gitignored as local generated output.

## Accomplished
-  Added live context-efficiency assist in `wf.ps1` for key commands (`status`, `health`, `start-session`, `review`, `audit`, `publish`, etc.).
-  Enabled orchestrator auto-action (`autoRunOnStartSessionWhenRed: true`) in `config/context-efficiency.json`.
-  Updated docs in `README.md` and `docs/guides/SESSION-GUIDE.md` to clarify auto + manual behavior.
-  Added `.audit/` to `.gitignore`.
-  Validated behavior by running `wf.ps1 start-session auto-compact-check` and confirming auto compact-start execution.
-  Committed and pushed all changes to `main` in commit `575ba04`.

## Next Steps
- Monitor `docs/sessions/metrics/context-usage.csv` next 3-5 sessions to confirm adoption improves above RED threshold.
- Optionally tune live-assist command list in `context-efficiency.json` if message frequency is too high/low.

## Relevant Files
- scripts/utilities/wf.ps1  live assist logic, auto compact-start trigger, and checkpoint parse fix.
- config/context-efficiency.json  liveAssist policy and auto-run flag enabled.
- README.md  runbook updates for auto context optimization behavior.
- docs/guides/SESSION-GUIDE.md  session-start automation guidance.
- .gitignore  local `.audit/` artifacts ignored.
- docs/sessions/metrics/context-usage.csv  telemetry updated with auto compact-start events.

observations.title: Identified validation and security defects in workspace-foundation review
observations.content:
**What**: Performed adversarial review of workspace-foundation and identified multiple actionable issues in project validation and event bus scripts.
**Why**: User requested Judge B blind review across correctness, edge cases, security, CI/governance reliability.
**Where**: C:\Workspace_local\workspace-foundation\scripts\validation\validate-project.ps1; C:\Workspace_local\workspace-foundation\scripts\utilities\event-bus.ps1; C:\Workspace_local\workspace-foundation\scripts\validation\validate-workspace.ps1
**Learned**: validate-project incorrectly parses Cargo.toml as JSON, misreports ahead/behind counts, and always runs npm typecheck even when only tsc exists; event-bus uses Invoke-Expression with unescaped payload/handler values, creating command-injection and quoting-failure risk.

observations.title: Hardened wf command execution and validator correctness
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed unsafe command execution and path/validation issues: removed `Invoke-Expression` usage in wf dispatch/events and event bus handler execution, corrected review script path resolution, fixed Rust TOML detection/typecheck command selection/ahead-behind mapping, and updated workspace git identity resolution plus stale README command references.
**Why**: Post-judgment triage identified real correctness and security risks in command execution and operational validation flows.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/wf.ps1, scripts/utilities/event-bus.ps1, scripts/validation/validate-project.ps1, scripts/validation/validate-workspace.ps1, scripts/diagnostics/validate-sdd-governance.ps1, README.md
**Learned**: Dynamic PSObject property access with variable event names must use `PSObject.Properties[...]` helpers to avoid silent handler misses; replacing string-eval with typed invocation removes injection class and stabilizes argument parsing.

observations.title: Fixed update-all paths and cloud connector auth/config behavior
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed `update-all` path resolution to use repo root, expanded script governance workflow config triggers, corrected cloud connector config path resolution, implemented provider-specific auth/header/body handling, and aligned cloud connector docs with actual behavior (including Bedrock SigV4 limitation note).
**Why**: Post-judgment triage identified real failures in update flow and cloud connector execution plus documentation drift.
**Where**: scripts/validation/update-all.ps1, .github/workflows/script-governance.yml, scripts/utilities/invoke-cloud-agent.ps1, docs/guides/CLOUD-AGENT-CONNECTOR.md
**Learned**: PowerShell interpolation with `?` after variable names requires `${var}` style to avoid malformed URI output; variable name collisions with script parameters (e.g., `$Config`) can cause parameter binding conversion errors.

observations.title: Found telemetry correlation integrity gap
observations.content:
**What**: Identified that telemetry consolidation can append token usage from unrelated runtime rows because runtime CSV has no Session_ID and consolidation uses latest row globally while stamping current session/user.
**Why**: User requested blind adversarial review focused on fabricated telemetry and correlation risks.
**Where**: workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/consolidate-telemetry.ps1, workspace-foundation/scripts/utilities/invoke-cloud-agent.ps1, workspace-foundation/skills/cloud-agent-connector-skill/SKILL.md
**Learned**: Even with CSV-cell sanitization in place, data-integrity failures can fabricate management telemetry if correlation keys are absent or optional.

observations.title: Bounded Judgment Day with user-controlled passes
observations.content:
**What**: Reworked `judgment-day.ps1` to default to max 3 passes, show per-pass results and suggestions, and ask user before additional pass (unless `-NoPrompt`). `wf.ps1 judgment-day` now invokes bounded mode by default (`-MaxPasses 3`).
**Why**: User requested anti-loop behavior with transparent pass-by-pass results and user confirmation for continuing analysis.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/judgment-day.ps1, scripts/utilities/wf.ps1, docs/guides/JUDGMENT-GUIDE.md
**Learned**: Quick scope from `invoke-judgment.ps1` can return success without critic/remediation; bounded wrapper must treat quick scope approval by exit code only to avoid false "attention required" loops.

observations.title: Set Judgment Day default to bounded 3-pass mode
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented bounded `judgment-day.ps1` with default max 3 passes, per-pass results/suggestions, optional user prompt for continuation, and non-blocking advisory handling for unconfigured external provider connectivity errors.
**Why**: User requested loop-safe judgment flow that remains actionable and interactive, without blocking on expected unconfigured connector credentials.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/judgment-day.ps1, scripts/utilities/wf.ps1, docs/guides/JUDGMENT-GUIDE.md
**Learned**: Quick scope should be approved by exit code alone; full scope still evaluates blocking signals, while auth/connection provider errors are downgraded to warnings in bounded orchestrator flow.

observations.title: Hardened cloud telemetry correlation and documentation alignment
observations.content:
**What**: Updated cloud connector and consolidation to avoid fabricated telemetry by requiring session correlation, adding Session_ID/Request_ID to runtime telemetry, stronger CSV sanitization, duplicate-skip behavior, and docs/skill alignment for proxy-signed Bedrock plus metadata-only local config.
**Why**: Post-judgment findings identified telemetry attribution drift, schema mismatch, and docs/runtime inconsistencies.
**Where**: scripts/utilities/invoke-cloud-agent.ps1, scripts/utilities/consolidate-telemetry.ps1, docs/guides/CLOUD-AGENT-CONNECTOR.md, docs/guides/cloud-agent-security.md, skills/cloud-agent-connector-skill/SKILL.md, .gitignore
**Learned**: Consolidation must fail-safe on missing correlated runtime rows; writing management telemetry from non-correlated data creates governance noise and false metrics.

observations.title: Session summary: workspace-foundation
observations.content:
## Goal
Finalize loop-safe judgment policy, clean and align cloud telemetry/docs behavior, validate with bounded judgment, publish, and close session.

## Instructions
- Keep default judgment passes at 3 (avoid looping).
- Show pass results and suggestions, ask user before continuing.
- Treat unconfigured external provider credential/connection issues as warning (non-blocking).
- Keep functionality manual-capable and documented.

## Discoveries
- Previous `judgment-day.ps1` always approved after one run and had no real pass-control; required full orchestration rewrite.
- Quick scope from `invoke-judgment.ps1` exits successfully without critic/remediation; bounded wrapper must treat this separately.
- Cloud telemetry consolidation can fabricate data unless session-correlated runtime rows are required and duplicates are prevented.
- Cloud docs and skill content had stale assumptions (local config as secrets, direct Bedrock SigV4 examples) inconsistent with runtime.

## Accomplished
-  Rewrote `scripts/utilities/judgment-day.ps1` with bounded 3-pass default, per-pass summaries, optional user continuation prompt, and no-loop behavior.
-  Updated `scripts/utilities/wf.ps1` to invoke bounded judgment mode by default.
-  Updated `docs/guides/JUDGMENT-GUIDE.md` with new pass policy and non-blocking provider-connection warning behavior.
-  Hardened `scripts/utilities/invoke-cloud-agent.ps1`: session/request telemetry fields, safer CSV handling, provider defaults, endpoint policy, metadata-only local config messaging.
-  Hardened `scripts/utilities/consolidate-telemetry.ps1`: strict correlation, duplicate guards, fail-safe skip on missing data, sanitized output.
-  Aligned cloud docs/skill files: `docs/guides/CLOUD-AGENT-CONNECTOR.md`, `docs/guides/cloud-agent-security.md`, `skills/cloud-agent-connector-skill/SKILL.md`, plus `.gitignore` runtime telemetry handling.
-  Ran bounded full judgment validation and got approved in pass 1.
-  Committed and pushed all final updates in commit `7603e86`.

## Next Steps
- Optionally add explicit `Session_ID` parameter to `invoke-cloud-agent.ps1` to avoid fallback to latest session artifact in concurrent environments.
- Add lightweight tests for telemetry schema/duplicate-skip/sanitization regression.
- Monitor next 2-3 judgment runs to confirm stable pass behavior and no false-blocking on provider auth warnings.

## Relevant Files
- scripts/utilities/judgment-day.ps1  bounded pass orchestration with user-driven continuation and advisory handling.
- scripts/utilities/wf.ps1  default judgment-day integration (`-MaxPasses 3`).
- docs/guides/JUDGMENT-GUIDE.md  updated default pass policy documentation.
- scripts/utilities/invoke-cloud-agent.ps1  runtime telemetry schema, sanitization, provider defaults, env loading behavior.
- scripts/utilities/consolidate-telemetry.ps1  correlated, non-fabricating management telemetry consolidation.
- docs/guides/CLOUD-AGENT-CONNECTOR.md  cloud connector setup/security/telemetry guidance aligned with runtime.
- docs/guides/cloud-agent-security.md  security model and provider notes aligned.
- skills/cloud-agent-connector-skill/SKILL.md  execution/security expectations aligned with script capabilities.
- .gitignore  runtime telemetry folder ignored.

observations.content:
**What**: Se realiz limpieza y homologacin total de documentacin, scripts y configuracin en Foundation - Development Stack, eliminando legacy, rutas y branding obsoletos. Todo qued alineado a skills, hooks, SDD, juicio adversarial y normativas vigentes.
**Why**: Solicitud de dejar el proyecto 100% consistente, sin residuos legacy, y reflejando el stack y polticas actuales.

**Learned**: Es fundamental registrar autorizaciones globales del usuario para evitar repreguntas innecesarias; el orquestador y subagentes deben optimizar validaciones y no pedir confirmacin si ya existe autorizacin explcita. Las normativas deben reflejar este flujo para mxima eficiencia.

observations.title: Normativa: evitar repreguntas tras autorizacin global
observations.content:

**Why**: Se detect que repreguntar ralentiza el flujo y contradice la intencin del usuario de mxima eficiencia y automatizacin.
**Where**: Normativas internas, skills de orquestacin, instrucciones de sesin.
**Learned**: Registrar explcitamente las autorizaciones globales y blanket approvals en memoria persistente; los agentes deben consultar este registro antes de solicitar confirmaciones adicionales.

observations.title: Leccin: consultar autorizaciones antes de repreguntar
observations.content:
**What**: Leccin aprendida: El orquestador y subagentes deben consultar memoria persistente de autorizaciones antes de solicitar validaciones al usuario.
**Why**: Para evitar repreguntas innecesarias y optimizar el flujo de trabajo, especialmente tras autorizaciones blanket/globales.
**Where**: Protocolo de orquestacin, skills de sesin, instrucciones de agentes.
**Learned**: Registrar y consultar autorizaciones en memoria persistente es clave para eficiencia y experiencia de usuario.

**Where**: tools/session-autostart.config.json (compactStartThreshold, autoCompactionThreshold), AGENTS.md (Pre-Compact Hook)
**Learned**: El valor 250 es el mnimo viable recomendado por anlisis tcnico

observations.title: Engram architecture analysis - session lifecycle
observations.content:
**What**: Anlisis completo de configuracin de Engram, backup y mecanismos de cache
**Why**: Evaluar si engram-backup-manager.ps1 es necesario o es un script redundante
**Where**: scripts/start-session.ps1, scripts/compact-start.ps1, scripts/context-pack.ps1, scripts/optimize-engram-usage.ps1
**Learned**: 
- Session start: context-pack  compact-start (optimiza contexto)
- Session end: day-end-closure  Engram save (persiste memoria)
- El backup actual solo guarda metadata, no contenido real de Engram
- Hay redundancia: optimize-engram-usage.ps1 no hace nada til

observations.title: Eliminated engram-backup, improved optimize-engram
observations.content:
**What**: Eliminados engram-backup-manager.ps1 (redundante) y mejorado optimize-engram-usage.ps1 con pre-carga real
**Why**: Anlisis arquitectnico mostr que backup solo guardaba metadata, day-end-closure ya persiste memoria real. Pre-carga de Engram trae benefit real.
**Where**: 
- scripts/monitoring/engram-backup-manager.ps1 - ELIMINADO
- scripts/utilities/optimize-engram-usage.ps1 - MEJORADO (pre-load real de Engram)
- tools/session-autostart.cmd - actualizado sin referencia a backup
- scripts/utilities/session-manual-end.cmd - actualizado
- scripts/README.md - documentacin actualizada
- docs/code-reviews/2026-04-21-002958-all-review.md - eliminado item obsoleto

**Learned**: 
- Backup al inicio no aporta si day-end-closure ya persiste contenido real
- Pre-load de Engram genera cache de observaciones para sesin
- Decisin: context-pack cache no necesario (benefit mnimo vs complejidad)

observations.title: Session summary: workspace_local
observations.content:
## Goal
Optimizar configuracin de Engram, eliminar scripts redundantes, mejorar pre-carga al inicio de sesin

## Instructions
- user decidi no implementar cache para context-pack (benefit mnimo vs complejidad)
- user solicit cierre completo de todas las tareas

## Discoveries
- engram-backup-manager.ps1 solo guardaba metadata, no contenido real
- day-end-closure ya hace backup real de memorias
- optimize-engram-usage.ps1 original estaba simulado (no haca nada)
- Pre-carga de Engram genera cache en .session/engram-cache/preload-cache.json
- Decisin: context-pack cache no necesario

## Accomplished
-  Eliminado engram-backup-manager.ps1
-  Mejorado optimize-engram-usage.ps1 con pre-carga real de Engram (context, observations, search)
-  Actualizado session-autostart.cmd (removida referencia a backup)
-  Actualizado session-manual-end.cmd (removida referencia a backup)
-  Actualizado scripts/README.md
-  Actualizado code-reviews doc (eliminado item obsoleto)
-  Testeado nuevo script de optimize-engram (funciona, 1.6s)
-  Threshold compact: 50250 tokens (correccin de inconsistencia)
-  Guardado en Engram: decisin arquitectnica

## Next Steps
- Ninguno pendiente de esta sesin

## Relevant Files
- scripts/utilities/optimize-engram-usage.ps1 - mejorado con pre-carga real
- scripts/monitoring/engram-backup-manager.ps1 - ELIMINADO
- tools/session-autostart.cmd - actualizado
- scripts/utilities/session-manual-end.cmd - actualizado
- scripts/README.md - documentacin actualizada

observations.title: Created simplify-text.ps1 for token efficiency
observations.content:
**What**: Creado simplify-text.ps1 para simplificacin de texto y reduccin de tokens
**Why**: Eliminar ruido en prompts (emojis, markdown, frases redundantes) para reducir consumo de tokens
**Where**: scripts/utilities/simplify-text.ps1 + wf.ps1 (comando simplify-text) + scripts/README.md

**Learned**:
- PowerShell tiene issues con unicode complejo (emojis con rangos)
- Solucin: usar pattern simple [^\\w\\s.,;:!?()''""-] que preserva acentos
- Reduccin tpica: 15-25% (5-10 tokens por prompt)
- Abbreviaciones: por favorpls, es importanteimp, en conclusionend

observations.content:
**What**: Hice openGentleman OS-agnstico usando wrapper `og.sh` que detecta Windows y delega a `og.ps1` automticamente.
**Why**: Para que usuarios de Windows no necesiten emuladores ni VMs para ejecutar el proyecto.
**Where**: openGentleman/scripts/og.sh, openGentleman/README.md
**Learned**: og.sh con `uname -s` puede detectar MINGW/MSYS y delegar a PowerShell.

observations.title: Homologated all openGentleman scripts to OS-agnostic
observations.content:
**What**: Actualic todos los scripts .sh principales de openGentleman para detectar Windows y delegar a .ps1 automticamente.
**Why**: Para que usuarios de Windows no necesiten emuladores ni recordar comandos especficos de Mac/Linux.
**Where**: openGentleman/scripts/og.sh, wf.sh, connect-dify.sh, sync-foundation.sh, handoff-compress.sh, auto-init-dev-environment.sh, cleanup-project.sh, setup.sh
**Learned**: El patrn es agregar `detect_os()` con `uname -s` y verificar MINGW/MSYS, luego delegar a `exec powershell` si existe el equivalente .ps1.

observations.title: Fixed script governance parser error
observations.content:
**What**: Corregido validate-script-governance.ps1 que fallaba por sintaxis PowerShell invlida
**Why**: El `[OK]` se interpret como expresin (ndice), causando parser error. Tambines/corrupted en lneas 1-3
**Where**: scripts/diagnostics/validate-script-governance.ps1
**Learned**: En PowerShell, `[OK]` sin comillas se parsea como operador de ndice  siempre usar Write-Output o comillas

observations.title: Integrated parser error governance rule
observations.content:
**What**: Governance rule integrada en validate-script-governance.ps1 para detectar automticamente `[OK]` al inicio de lnea que causa parser error
**Why**: Evitar que el error se replique y asegurar validacin automtica en CI/hooks. El patrn `[OK]` al inicio de lnea fuera de funcin o here-stringparsea como expresin en PowerShell, causando error
**Where**: scripts/diagnostics/validate-script-governance.ps1 - ahora detecta y falla antes de push si encuentra el patrn invlido
**Learned**: SOLUCIN: la regla es ahora nativa del governance. No requiere buscar Engram - el orquestador ejecutar esta validacin automticamente

- Sistema multilinge ES/PT/EN implementado

observations.title: Session summary: gentleman-foundation
observations.content:
## Goal
Implement MCP Bridge adapter layer, token consumption/context reporting, and real-time executive dashboard for Foundation workspace to make all improvements, efficiency, optimization available to ALL AI tools (not just opencode/vscode/cline).

## Instructions
User required: all tools/IDEs/plugins compatibility, automated, homologated, agnostic, functional, ordered, updated both local and repo (develop & main). Also needed on-demand token consumption/utility/context reporting process, and real-time governance/audit process for management.

## Discoveries
- MCP Bridge is optimal solution covering 80% of use cases via standard MCP protocol.

observations.title: Pre-close validator implemented for session closure
observations.content:
**What**: Created comprehensive pre-close-validator.ps1 that validates git state, pending tasks, partial implementations, session locks, and Engram health before allowing session closure

**Why**: User requested assurance that no pending work, partial implementations, or misunderstandings remain for next session. Wanted automatic resolution of issues.

**Where**: tools/pre-close-validator.ps1, tools/session-manager.ps1, tools/session-manual-end.cmd, scripts/utilities/SESSION-MANAGEMENT/end-session.ps1, AGENTS.md

**Learned**: 
- PowerShell string interpolation with colons requires -f format operator
- Git push syntax needs explicit branch name, not HEAD:branch format
- Engram health check triggers GitHub API rate limiting (non-critical)
- Critical TODO/FIXME detection should only scan actual code files, not documentation
- Session closure now blocked until all validations pass or -Force used

**Features**:
- Auto-resolve git issues (auto-commit, auto-push)
- Detects critical pending tasks in code files
- Checks for session locks
- Non-blocking Engram validation
- Integrated into all session closure paths

- No sensitive code/doc content in logs (metadata only)

- PowerShell scripts must use ArrayList instead of += for collections

- Error: `Method invocation failed because [System.Management.Automation.PSObject] does not contain a method named 'op_Addition'` - fixed by using ArrayList
- Error: `$(subexpression) is missing the closing ')'` - fixed by breaking complex expressions into variables
- Error: `elseif missing closing ')'` - fixed by restructuring if/elseif/else blocks
- Backup creates `.enc` files in `.backups/` (encrypted with AES-256)
- Drift detection compares `LastWriteTime` of code files vs documentation files
